market_summary: REGIME: RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE — SPY close=645.09, below BOTH MA50=678.97 and MA200=657.77 (4th+ consecutive session below MA200, confirming entrenched regime deterioration). QQQ below both MAs (1m=-6.96%, 3m=-7.77%). IWM above MA200 only. TLT below both MAs (mom=-1.41%) — bond bid fully absent, stagflation/rates-pressure macro backdrop persists. BTC-USD mom=-22.23%, 3m=-23.11%, below MA200 — crypto fully risk-off. GLD close=400.64, above MA200=376.10 (buffer=+6.5%) but sharply below MA50=455.19; 1m=-15.37%, 3m=-3.14% — precious metals correction deepening. Portfolio weighted avg momentum ~25% vs SPY mom=-0.28% — +25pp alpha spread maintained. Regime: RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE, no upgrade warranted. April 2 reciprocal tariff deadline remains dominant near-term binary tail risk.

LEADERSHIP & THESIS DAMAGE: XOM leads (#1, mom=+35.96%, 3m=+39.45%, above BOTH MA50=147.71 and MA200=120.96, dd63=-5.48%, 1m=+10.98%) — cleanest holding, no flags, Q1-FY2026 EPS=$1.68 ramping to $2.00 by Q3-2026. LMT (#4, mom=+26.14%, 3m=+30.69%, above MA200, 1m=-2.60%, dd63=-9.83%) — defense rearmament thesis intact, EPS Q1=$6.72→Q4=$8.45. GOOGL (#6, mom=+14.59%, 3m=-10.57%, 1m=-10.16%, below MA50, above MA200=261.95, dd63=-18.21%) — persistent negative 3m momentum, EPS Q1=$2.76 growing slowly to $3.21 by Q4; still weakest equity thesis. DAMAGE FLAGS: GLD 1m=-15.37%, 3m=-3.14%, dd63=-19.21%, MA200 buffer compressed to +6.5% ($400.64 vs $376.10) — buffer narrowing dangerously; pre-committed kill-switch at MA200 breach remains active. SLV 1m=-24.08%, 3m=-6.28%, dd63=-42.45%, vol20=61.04%, below MA50 by -21.4% — most severe drawdown in portfolio; structural 12m=+98.72% and MA200 buffer (+16.8%) intact but technical damage acute.

GO/NO-GO: NON-REBALANCE DAY — Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 2 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible window ~2026-04-01). No emergency kill-switch breach: GLD close=$400.64 vs MA200=$376.10 buffer=+6.5% — above floor but the narrowest buffer in this run series; GLD is the #1 monitoring priority into next rebalance. SLV close=$60.77 vs MA200=$52.02 buffer=+16.8% — above floor. TSM above MA200=$285.92, buffer=+14.1% — above emergency exit level. All five equity EPS acceleration trajectories confirmed via qualitative refresh: XOM Q1=$1.68→Q3=$2.00; CVX Q1=$1.69→Q2-Q3=$2.12; KLAC Q1=$9.14→Q4=$12.10; LMT Q1=$6.72→Q4=$8.45; GOOGL Q1=$2.76→Q4=$3.21. DO NOTHING — hold portfolio exactly unchanged. Pre-logging for next rebalance (~2026-04-01): (1) GLD MA200 buffer at +6.5% — if buffer compresses below +5% before next scheduled rebalance, emergency review is activated; (2) GOOGL 3m=-10.57% persistent — primary replacement candidate evaluation required at next rebalance; (3) SLV dd63=-42.45% remains structurally damaged; (4) TSM 1m=-15.66% — size review at next rebalance if MA50 breach persists.

thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 2 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible window ~2026-04-01). The portfolio completed a material 4-action rebalance on 2026-03-25, repositioning into deeper Energy/Defense overweight (XOM=22%, CVX=18%) at the expense of GLD/SLV/GOOGL. Allowing 5+ additional days for this positioning to season before next evaluation is consistent with the monthly-hold strategy mandate and avoids excessive turnover drag.

REGIME CONFIRMATION: SPY remains below MA200 for 4th+ consecutive session (close=645.09 vs MA200=657.77, gap=-1.9%), consistent with RISK-OFF/DEFENSIVE regime already priced into current portfolio construction. The portfolio's defensive tilt (Energy 40% + Defense 10% + Gold 22% + Silver 2%) is appropriate and aligned with the regime. The only incremental deterioration vs yesterday is GLD's MA200 buffer compressing further to +6.5% (from ~+7% yesterday) — not yet a kill-switch breach but elevated monitoring required.

FACTOR STABILITY: All 8 holdings retain positive momentum composites (XOM=+35.96%, KLAC=+36.77%, CVX=+29.09%, SLV=+30.25%, LMT=+26.14%, TSM=+23.45%, GOOGL=+14.59%, GLD=+10.51%). Qualitative refresh confirms EPS acceleration intact across all five equities. No kill-switch breaches confirmed. The cost of acting today (20-30bps round-trip turnover drag, disruption of factor seasonality, zero incremental expected alpha) far exceeds any marginal benefit. PRE-LOG for 2026-04-01 rebalance: GLD trim evaluation (22%→15% if MA200 buffer <5%), GOOGL replacement evaluation (CVX/LMT add), TSM size review.
risks: MACRO: April 2 reciprocal tariff deadline is the dominant near-term binary event. A tariff escalation scenario could pressure TSM (Taiwan ADR, direct tariff/geopolitical target, 1m=-15.66% already) and KLAC (semis, China export restriction overhang). Energy (XOM/CVX at 40% combined) faces oil demand destruction risk if tariffs trigger a recession pricing episode. SPY MA200 breach now in 4th+ consecutive session — if SPY drops an additional -3% to ~$626, mandatory full defensive review is activated regardless of rebalance cadence.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: GLD MA200 buffer has narrowed to +6.5% ($400.64 vs $376.10) — the most critical monitoring item. A -7% move in GLD would breach MA200 and trigger the pre-committed emergency trim (from 22% to 10-15%). SLV vol20=61.04% means daily NAV contribution from 2% weight is amplified; dd63=-42.45% is persistent but MA200 buffer intact. GOOGL antitrust DOJ remedies (potential Chrome/Android divestiture order) remain a headline risk as court proceedings continue into April 2026.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: All 8 holdings highly liquid. No binary earnings events for XOM, CVX, KLAC, or LMT in the next 7 days. TSM faces the April 2 tariff binary event — existing 10% weight sized appropriately for this binary risk. No action to execute today per rebalance cadence policy.

NEXT REBALANCE TRIGGERS (~2026-04-01): (1) GOOGL 3m momentum must turn positive OR GOOGL is replaced — primary candidates: add CVX (weight 18%→20-22%) or add LMT (weight 10%→12-14%); (2) GLD sizing review: if MA200 buffer <5% at rebalance date, trim to 15%; if buffer >10% and recovering, hold at 22%; (3) TSM: if still below MA50 on rebalance date with 1m<-10%, size down to 7-8%; (4) Portfolio alpha spread vs SPY must remain above +20pp at next rebalance — currently +25pp (portfolio composite mom ~+25% vs SPY mom=-0.28%).
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case (100%): Mandatory non-action day per rebalance cadence policy (days since last rebalance checkpoint = 2, minimum spacing = 7 days). The prior rebalance on 2026-03-25 executed 4 changes (GLD trim 29%→22%, SLV trim 4%→2%, GOOGL trim 8%→6%, proceeds to XOM 15%→22% and CVX 14%→18%) that repositioned the portfolio appropriately for the RISK-OFF regime. The current portfolio shows +25pp alpha spread vs SPY, intact EPS trajectories across all 5 equities, no kill-switch breaches, and all 8 holdings above MA200. Estimated cost of unnecessary turnover today: 20-30bps round-trip with zero incremental expected alpha. The dominant forward risk is the April 2 tariff binary event — acting today increases rather than decreases regime exposure uncertainty. Expected edge of doing nothing: preserving full compounding of the 2026-03-25 rebalance through its intended holding window, with the next clean evaluation point at ~2026-04-01.
